title: Long Walk, Short Mate
setup: White's a-pawn is hanging back, but the b-rook has a clear lane to the 8th rank.
hint: Walk the rook to b8. The pawn structure does the rest.
explanation:
## The Idea

The presence of an extra White pawn on a2 is irrelevant — pure positional features (back-rank weakness + king without luft) decide the game. {{Rb8#}} is mate at once:

1. The rook arrives on the 8th rank with check.
2. The king on g8 is sealed by its own pawns on f7-g7-h7.
3. Squares **a8** through **h8** outside the king's bubble are all attacked by the rook.
4. Nothing recaptures, nothing blocks.

### Why "extra material" doesn't save Black

Black's king position is so weak that material is meaningless. **A single tempo of {{h6}} earlier in the game would have prevented this entire pattern**, regardless of how many pawns each side has.

> Material counts always come second to king safety in tactical positions.
